title: Rootful Podman NFS volume mount fails with chmod error
body:
### Issue Description

When trying to mount a volume from nfs share, I was getting this [error](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20801) before fixing nfs directory owner uid and gid. 

So I run chown on related directory at NFS server using uid and gid from docker image.

Mounting the volume again fails with a different (very similar) error. Now chmod fails now instead of chown. The permissions was set also mirroring the ones from docker image, still no success.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. have a directory on nfs share with id, gid and permissions matching directory from docker image 
2. run podman container with volume mount that uses the directory from nfs


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: chmod /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/systemd-zabbix-mysql-data/_data: operation not permitted
``` 

### Describe the results you expected

Volume mounts successfully

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Raspberry Pi OS Lite 64 bit (Debian 13)

### Additional information

directory ls data:
```sh
drwxrwxrwt    2 999      999           4096 Nov 30 16:07 mysql
```

container quadlet (zabbix-mysql.container):
```ini

[Unit]
Description=Zabbix Mysql Container

[Container]
AutoUpdate=registry
EnvironmentFile=/etc/zabbix-mysql.conf
Image=docker.io/mysql:8.0-oracle 
Volume=zabbix-mysql-data.volume:/var/lib/mysql/
Network=zabbix-net.network
Exec=--character-set-server=utf8 --collation-server=utf8_bin --default-authentication-plugin=mysql_native_password
User=999
Group=999

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

volume quadlet (zabbix-mysql-data.volume):
```ini
[Unit]
Description=A data volume for Zabbix Mysql Container

[Volume]
Device=192.168.1.1:/srv/raspberry/data/mysql
Type=nfs
Options=async
```

comments.body:
@LogExE  I would be recommending you to update podman to latest Version: 5.7.0 
if I wouldn't experience similar issue on it (rootful too) with https://github.com/yiisoft/yii2-app-basic 

> Failed to change permissions for directory "/app/runtime/logs": chmod(): Operation not permitted 
(by www-data) 

docker handles it without any problems
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
